name: Manage Multiple Python Versions with pyenv
description: Install and switch between multiple Python versions on the same machine using pyenv, with per-project version pinning.
prerequisites: macOS, Linux, or WSL (pyenv does not run natively on Windows)

steps.title: Install pyenv
steps.description: Install pyenv and its plugins (pyenv-virtualenv, pyenv-update). The installer adds pyenv to your home directory at ~/.pyenv.
steps.warning: After installation, you must add pyenv to your shell PATH. Follow the instructions printed by the installer to update your .bashrc or .zshrc.

steps.title: List available Python versions
steps.description: Show all available CPython 3.x versions that can be installed. The list includes stable releases and pre-release versions.
steps.tip: Look for the latest stable version (e.g., 3.12.x or 3.13.x) without a, b, or rc suffixes.

steps.title: Install a specific Python version
steps.description: Download and compile the specified Python version. It is installed to ~/.pyenv/versions/ and does not affect your system Python.

steps.title: Set the global default Python version
steps.description: Set the default Python version used in all shells. This writes to ~/.pyenv/version.

steps.title: Set a per-project Python version
steps.description: Pin a specific Python version for the current directory by creating a .python-version file. pyenv automatically switches versions when you enter this directory.
steps.tip: Commit .python-version to your repository so all team members use the same Python version.

steps.title: Verify the active Python version
steps.description: Display which Python version pyenv has selected and confirm that the python command resolves to it.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# pyenv setup and Python version management
set -e

# Install pyenv if not present
if ! command -v pyenv &> /dev/null; then
  curl https://pyenv.run | bash
  export PATH="$HOME/.pyenv/bin:$PATH"
  eval "$(pyenv init -)"
fi

PYTHON_VERSION=${1:-3.12.7}

echo "Installing Python $PYTHON_VERSION..."
pyenv install -s "$PYTHON_VERSION"
pyenv global "$PYTHON_VERSION"

echo "Active Python:"
pyenv version
python --version
pip --version

echo "pyenv setup complete with Python $PYTHON_VERSION."

faq.question: Does pyenv work on Windows?
faq.answer: pyenv does not run natively on Windows. Use pyenv-win (a separate port) or run pyenv inside WSL. Alternatively, use uv which manages Python versions cross-platform.

faq.question: What is the difference between pyenv global, local, and shell?
faq.answer: global sets the default version for your user (~/.pyenv/version). local pins a version to a directory (.python-version file). shell sets the version for the current terminal session only via the PYENV_VERSION environment variable.

faq.question: Can I use pyenv with virtual environments?
faq.answer: Yes. Install your desired Python version with pyenv, then create a venv with 'python -m venv .venv'. The venv will use whichever Python version pyenv has active. The pyenv-virtualenv plugin also provides direct integration.
